purpose: Test whether the pinned SparseDrive plan on the existing real source window causally depends on RGB and on the declared ego forward velocity. This does not test semantic correctness or simulator credibility.
held_fixed: six-camera ordering except inside the declared RGB intervention
source timestamps, calibration, camera-rig poses and navigation command
SparseDrive source, compatibility patch, checkpoint and reset procedure
four-frame warm-up and native six-step planning output

rgb_spatial_information.control: replace every camera pixel with the rounded ImageNet normalization mean

rgb_temporal_history.control: repeat the first source RGB frame at all four logical timestamps

primary_observation: fully warmed native six-point planning trajectory at source frame 30
repeat_reference: independent reset with identical RGB, state, calibration, pose and command

decision_rules.rgb_stream_influence: accepted only if the constant-RGB warmed-plan maximum absolute coordinate change exceeds the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.visual_history_influence: accepted only if the frozen-history warmed-plan maximum absolute coordinate change exceeds the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.ego_velocity_influence: accepted only if both plus and minus 2.0 m/s warmed-plan changes exceed the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.shortcut_or_semantic_correctness: not decidable from effect magnitude; report only as down-weighted or rejected overclaim

descriptive_diagnostics: fully warmed plan ADE and endpoint difference from baseline
plan ADE to a constant-velocity extrapolation formed from each supplied ego status
strongest_allowed_claim: On this one real, near-straight, four-frame input slice, a declared RGB or ego-status intervention did or did not causally change the frozen SparseDrive native plan beyond paired numerical repeat sensitivity.
forbidden_claims: RGB sensitivity proves correct object, lane or risk semantics
a plausible plan under removed RGB proves a safe fallback
the larger effect from non-commensurate RGB and velocity interventions proves feature dominance
the result qualifies HUGSIM or establishes SparseDrive safety
one slice rules out learned shortcuts in general
